name: d3-data-visualizer
description: Asks the user what data to analyse, designs the optimal visualization approach, then generates a self-contained HTML file using D3.js from CDN with embedded HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

gather-requirements.do: Use the AskUserQuestion tool to ask the user what data they want to visualize. Ask them to describe the data (or point to a file/URL), what kind of insights or story they want the visualization to convey, and any preferences for chart type or style. If they reference a file, read it to understand the data shape (columns, types, row count, value ranges). Summarize the data characteristics and the user's goals clearly.
gather-requirements.produces: A clear description of the data (structure, types, sample values, size), the user's visualization goals, and any stated preferences

design-visualization.do: Based on the data characteristics and user goals, design the optimal D3.js visualization approach. Consider the data types (categorical, temporal, numerical, hierarchical, network) and choose the most effective chart type (bar, line, scatter, area, pie, treemap, force-directed graph, heatmap, etc.). Specify: (1) the chart type and why it's the best fit, (2) which data fields map to which visual encodings (x-axis, y-axis, color, size, etc.), (3) any data transformations needed (aggregation, filtering, sorting), (4) interactive features (tooltips, zoom, hover effects, transitions), (5) color scheme and styling approach. If the data or goals are ambiguous, use AskUserQuestion to clarify before finalizing the design.
design-visualization.produces: A detailed visualization design specification: chart type, data-to-visual mappings, transformations, interactivity plan, color scheme, and layout approach

generate-html.do: Implement the visualization design as a single self-contained HTML file. The file must: (1) load D3.js from CDN (https://d3js.org/d3.v7.min.js) via a <script> tag, (2) embed all CSS in a <style> block with a clean, modern design (proper fonts, spacing, responsive container), (3) embed all JavaScript in a <script> block, (4) embed the data directly in the JavaScript (as a JSON array or appropriate structure) — do NOT reference external data files, (5) include interactive features from the design (tooltips, hover effects, smooth transitions), (6) include a title, axis labels, and a legend where appropriate, (7) be responsive and look good at common screen widths. Write the file to the current working directory with a descriptive filename like 'visualization.html'. Do NOT call the d3_data_visualizer tool — implement the visualization directly in this step.
generate-html.produces: The absolute file path of the generated self-contained HTML file

summary.do: Provide a concise summary to the user: (1) what data was visualized, (2) the chart type chosen and why, (3) key interactive features included, (4) the file path of the generated HTML file, and (5) instructions to open it (e.g., 'open visualization.html in a browser'). If anything could be improved or extended, mention one or two brief suggestions.
summary.produces: A user-friendly summary of the completed visualization with file path and usage instructions
